diagrams.title: Dijkstra Algorithm Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step execution of Dijkstra's algorithm on a sample graph with 5 nodes
elements: Source node S initially at distance 0
Each extraction marks vertex as finalized
Relaxation updates shorter paths through newly extracted vertex
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the greedy choice property ensures correctness by showing distance propagation through the graph

diagrams.title: Failure Case with Negative Weights
diagrams.description: Counterexample graph where Dijkstra fails: Source A connects to B (weight 2) and C (weight 5), C connects to B (weight -3)
elements: Dijkstra incorrectly finalizes B at distance 2
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why non-negative weight condition is essential for Dijkstra's correctness

diagrams.title: Comparison: Dijkstra vs Bellman-Ford vs Floyd-Warshall
diagrams.description: Table comparing three shortest path algorithms on multiple dimensions
elements: Dijkstra: Greedy, O((V+E)logV), no negative weights
Bellman-Ford: DP, O(VE), handles negative weights, detects negative cycles
Floyd-Warshall: DP, O(V³), all-pairs, handles negative weights
diagrams.purpose: Help students understand when to use each algorithm based on graph characteristics
